title: Executive Order 10903—Delegating Authority of the President With Respect to Regulations Relating to Certain Allowances and Benefits to Government Personnel on Overseas Duty

flags.justification: Each delegated authority is narrow and specific, tied to named statutes governing overseas personnel allowances — routine, bounded regulatory delegation.
Extensive, precise statutory citations throughout provide clear, traceable authority for each provision.
Many distinct provisions, but all coherently relate to one subject: overseas government personnel allowances and benefits.
Internal government personnel benefit regulations, not a compliance regime against third parties.
No compliance regime is created by this order.

Standing-procedural-framework category applies: this order itself revokes and consolidates roughly a dozen prior orders (10000, 10011, 10085, 10100, 10187, 10261, 10313, 10391, 10503, 10623, 10636), directly demonstrating an ongoing pattern of periodic revision.
Sec. 6 requires Federal Register publication — a transparency-preserving feature, not a removal of oversight.
No third-party compliance regime is created.
Routine administrative consolidation of existing personnel-benefit delegations; no novel authority claim.
A delegation from the President to the Secretary of State; no other agency's competing mandate is implicated.
No coverage population or exemption structure is created.

notes: Validation batch (positive). Structural weight: 0/12 = 0.0%. Dense, highly technical consolidation of overseas personnel-benefit delegations; last Eisenhower-era order in this batch.
